name: Alguerese (l'alguerès, Catalan of Alghero, Sardinia)

ancestors.notes: Catalan: Alguerese (l'alguerès) is the Catalan of Alghero (l'Alguer), a linguistic island on the north-west coast of Sardinia settled from the 14th c. under the Crown of Aragon. It belongs genetically to the Eastern Catalan bloc but, uniquely within it, has NO neutral vowel [ə]: unstressed /a/ and /e/ are not reduced to schwa but realised as an [a]-like vowel (pare → [ˈpaɾa]-type). Only the o/u → [u] reduction of Eastern Catalan applies.

Sardinian (Logudorese/Sassarese) adstrate: the dominant contact language of the island. Held responsible for the hallmark alguerès rhotacism — intervocalic and coda /d/ and /l/ → alveolar tap [ɾ] (rodó [ruˈɾo], ungla → [ˈuŋɡɾa], moneda → munera) — and for the acoustically atypical, less-open mid vowels (Recasens 1996 on centralised /e/) and heavy lexical borrowing.
Italian adstrate/roof language: the state language of Sardinia, source of recent borrowing and of pressure on the phonological system (metathesis, assimilation, epenthetic final -[i]).

notes: Alguerese (alguerès, català de l'Alguer) — the isolated Eastern-Catalan variety of Alghero (l'Alguer) on Sardinia, the only Catalan-speaking enclave in Italy, dating to the 14th-c. Catalano-Aragonese repopulation. MODELLED DELTAS over the Catalan (ca) base: (1) NO SCHWA REDUCTION: unlike the rest of Eastern Catalan, alguerès does NOT reduce unstressed /a/,/e/ to [ə] — the nucleus_unstressed positions of ⟨a⟩ and ⟨e⟩ are overridden back to [a]/[e]. The o/u → [u] reduction IS kept (inherited from ca). (2) /v/ RETAINED: ⟨v⟩ = [v] distinct from /b/ — alguerès keeps the B–V opposition (a conservative, Balearic/Valencian-like trait), so the ca-default betacism ⟨v⟩=[b] is overridden. (3) RHOTACISM — the signature alguerès feature: intervocalic and coda /d/ and /l/ surface as an alveolar tap [ɾ] (vila [ˈbiɾa]-type, escola, cada [ˈkaɾa]), attributed to Sardinian contact; encoded in positional_graphemes and folded into the /d/,/l/ allophone sets so the inventory stays closed. (4) DESPALATALISATION OF CODA ⟨ll⟩: word-final/coda ⟨ll⟩ → [l] (vermell [valˈmel], Pou Vell [pow ˈvel]) — the orthographic ⟨ll⟩ is retained but coda /ʎ/ depalatalises. INHERITED from ca and NOT re-declared: prepalatal ⟨x⟩/⟨ix⟩ = [ʃ], ⟨j⟩/⟨g⟩(e,i) = [ʒ], the o→[u] reduction, final-obstruent devoicing, and the seven-quality tonic vowel set (with the caveat that alguerès mid vowels è/ò are acoustically less open than Central Catalan — documented, not re-mapped, since the phoneme identities are unchanged). DOCUMENTED-NOT-MODELLED (out of grapheme→IPA scope or too lexically conditioned to map cleanly): epenthetic final -[i] (acud [aˈkuɾi]), metathesis/assimilation, and the strong Sardinian/Italian lexical layer. Provenance honesty: base inherited from the research-tier ca spec; the deltas are page-pinned to the deposited OA studies (Bosch; Zurita Saballs; the Cervantes-Virtual acoustic vowel study), with Kuen (1932) reached only via those sources.

sources.author: Bosch i Rodoreda, A.
sources.title: Problemes de codificació de l'alguerès
sources.notes: Read directly (papers/iberian/bosch_problemes_codificacio_algueres.pdf). Rhotacism -/d/- > [ɾ] (rodó [ruˈɾo], rumpuda) and -/l/- > [ɾ] (ungla→ungra); despalatalisation of coda -ll → [l] (vermell [valˈmel]); epenthetic final -[i] (acud [aˈkuɾi]).

sources.author: Zurita Saballs, J.
sources.title: La llengua literària catalana a l'Alguer (TFG)
sources.notes: Read (papers/iberian/algueres_llengua_literaria_zurita.pdf). p.19: 7-vowel tonic system, LACKS the neutral vowel despite Eastern-Catalan membership; unstressed a/e realised [a]-like (para for pare). p.~26: 'tot i ser considerat del bloc del català oriental, no té la vocal neutra'; alguerès keeps the B/V distinction phonetically.

sources.author: (Cervantes Virtual, acoustic study)
sources.title: Sobre la correcta interpretació dels fonemes vocàlics de l'alguerès: un estudi acústic
sources.notes: Read (papers/iberian/algueres_vocals_estudi_acustic.pdf). Publication year approximate. Mid vowels acoustically atypical vs Central Catalan: /e/ centralised/posterior (citing Recasens 1996 p.70); open-mid è/ò less open than Central Catalan, causing non-alguerès transcribers to mis-classify. Documents the vowel qualities but confirms the phoneme identities used here.

sources.author: Kuen, H.
sources.title: El dialecto de Alguer y su posición en la historia de la lengua catalana
sources.publisher: Anuari de l'Oficina Romànica de Lingüística i Literatura V-VII
sources.notes: The classic monograph on Alguerese. Not open; its data are quoted extensively in the acoustic study and in Recasens, the OA sources used here.
